title: Wrong Join Result under the condition of <=> and NULL
description:
## Bug Report

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)

```
CREATE TABLE t0(c0 int);
CREATE TABLE t1 LIKE t0;
INSERT INTO t1 VALUES (NULL);
REPLACE INTO t1 VALUES (NULL);
REPLACE INTO t0(c0) VALUES (NULL);

SELECT * FROM  t0 INNER JOIN t1 ON ((((t1.c0)<=>(t0.c0)))AND(((((NULL)>(1))) IS NULL)));

SELECT * FROM  t0 INNER JOIN t1 ON ((((t1.c0)<=>(t0.c0)))AND(((((t1.c0)>(1))) IS NULL)));
```

### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)

The second query should behave same with the first query.
The result of the second query should be:
```
+------+------+
| c0   | c0   |
+------+------+
| NULL | NULL |
| NULL | NULL |
+------+------+
```

### 3. What did you see instead (Required)
But the second result produce the following wrong result, which also does not conform to MySQL.
```
Empty set (0.00 sec)
```
### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)
8.0.11-TiDB-v8.5.1 TiDB
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t2(c1 int, c2 varchar(10));
SELECT * FROM t2 INNER JOIN t3 ON (t2.c1 = t3.c3 OR (t2.c1 IS NULL AND t3.c3 IS NULL)) AND (t2.c1 IS NULL);
key_bug_features: INNER JOIN with NULL comparison using <=>
